title: Moral Epistles 65: The Five Causes
description: Seneca synthesizes Plato, Aristotle, and the Stoics on the causes of existence — matter, form, agent, purpose, and the Idea.
introduction: Seneca synthesizes Plato, Aristotle, and the Stoics on the causes of existence — matter, form, agent, purpose, and the Idea.

translation.original_text: Quinque ergo causae sunt, ut Plato dicit: id ex quo, id a quo, id in quo, id ad quod, id propter quod.
translation.text: There are therefore five causes, as Plato says: that from which, that by which, that in which, that toward which, and that on account of which.
